title: MinerU
summary: Document parsing engine with three-backend orchestration (pipeline/vlm/hybrid), scored multi-GPU router, async task state machine, and sliding-window memory bounding. Exemplary structured output specification and env-var catalog in docs. Severe test thinness (1 e2e test, 0.2% coverage floor) and unarmored HTTP defaults (no auth, no rate limit, no upload cap) block adoption. Value is in pattern/knowledge extraction, not dependency use. AGPL-3.0 license is an additional adoption blocker.
creator_view: MinerU understands document parsing as an orchestration problem, not a model problem — three backends, one router, three install profiles, one structured output contract. The Creator View value is in three items: (1) the POST /tasks async task state machine which is a direct answer to the T28 progressive-extraction gap, (2) the output_files.md structured-output specification which is a template for SoNash's missing ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md, (3) the env var catalog in cli_tools.md which is a template for consolidating SoNash's scattered SKIP_*/GSD_*/CAS_* env vars. Test infrastructure and HTTP security are anti-patterns worth filing to guard against.

candidates.name: Structured output contract page
candidates.description: docs/en/reference/output_files.md is an exemplary human-readable output spec: typed fields, enumerated block types, breaking-change callouts. Template for creating docs/agent_docs/ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md to mirror SoNash CAS v3.0 schema.

candidates.name: Environment variable catalog
candidates.description: docs/en/usage/cli_tools.md has prose catalog of 15+ MINERU_* env vars. Template for docs/agent_docs/ENV_VARS.md to consolidate SoNash scattered SKIP_*/GSD_*/CAS_*/ANALYZE_* across hooks, scripts, and skills.

candidates.name: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup (shape reference only)
candidates.description: mineru/cli/fast_api.py implements POST /tasks with four states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue, semaphore concurrency cap, background TTL sweep. CORRECTION from first pass: for T28 progressive extraction, port from crawl4ai (Apache-2.0, persistent resume_state) not MinerU (AGPL, in-memory). MinerU is a shape reference only.

candidates.name: Backend decision matrix for multi-mode tools
candidates.description: docs/en/quick_start/index.md has pre-install decision matrix: 5 backends × 8 constraints. Apply to /analyze and /media-analysis routing docs where users pick source type + depth + backend.

candidates.name: Wrap upstream CLI instead of reimplementing
candidates.description: mineru/model/vlm/vllm_server.py is 70 lines wrapping vllm.entrypoints.cli.main. Intercepts sys.argv, parses out --model/--port/--gpu-memory-utilization/--logits-processors, applies mod_kwargs_by_device_type, auto-injects MinerULogitsProcessor, sets OMP_NUM_THREADS=1, hands off to vllm_main(). Cheap maintainable alternative to forking. Applicable to SoNash for wrapping third-party CLIs.

candidates.name: Coarse-to-fine two-stage parsing framing
candidates.description: MinerU 2.5 paper (arxiv 2509.22186) introduces a 1.2B VLM with stage-1 layout analysis on downsampled images + stage-2 content recognition on native-resolution crops. Framing analogue for SoNash depth tiers (Quick -> Standard -> Deep).

candidates.name: README integration claim unbacked by first-party code
candidates.description: README lists MCP Server integration but opendatalab/mineru-mcp does not exist (404). Only community implementations exist (top: neosun100/mineru-mcp-server, 9 stars, no license). CORRECTION from first pass where this was treated as a T2 knowledge candidate. Guard: SoNash must distinguish first-party from community integrations in claims.

adoption.notes: AGPL + test thinness + unarmored HTTP block adoption

creatorLens: MinerU 3.0.0 is an orchestration-first document parsing engine. Three backends, one router, async task state machine, sliding-window memory bounding, layered install recipes, and an exemplary structured output spec. Test and HTTP security are where it falls down — one fuzzy-match e2e test and no API auth/rate-limit defaults. Extract patterns and documentation discipline, not code (AGPL).

context.t28Relevance: POST /tasks async task API is the direct answer to Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive/resumable extraction). output_files.md is template for missing ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_CONTRACT.md. env-var catalog is template for missing ENV_VARS.md. All three are T1 extraction candidates that connect to existing in-flight SoNash work.
notablePatterns: Three-backend dispatch with deferred importlib imports and duck-typed signatures
Async task state machine (POST /tasks): four states, asyncio.Queue, semaphore, TTL sweep
Scored multi-GPU router: (queued + processing + pending) / max_concurrent with jitter
Sliding window for long-doc memory bounding (default size 64, uniform across all backends)
Thread-safety: threading.RLock on keyed singleton, atexit cleanup
Structured output spec as first-class product artifact (middle.json schema)
Prose environment variable catalog (15+ MINERU_* vars)
Hardware decision matrix (5 backends × 8 constraints) pre-install
Changelog-as-capability-index with CVE refs and quantified perf deltas
antiPatterns: Single fuzzy-match e2e test with 0.2% coverage floor
Silent retry workflow converts flake into green checkmarks
HTTP API unarmored: no auth, no rate limit, no upload cap, no CORS/TrustedHost, docs enabled by default
SSRF primitive in user-controlled server_url field
i18n parity broken: 13 Chinese-only chip docs with no English equivalents
FAQ defers most questions to external AI assistant

priorAnalysis.notes: Quality score dropped from 85 (quick) to 74 (standard) after reading source — test infrastructure and HTTP hardening weren't visible in the API-only scan. Personal fit score rose from 50 to 62 after finding three T1-tier extraction candidates.
